Title: Defense-related transcription factors WRKY70 and WRKY54 modulate osmotic stress tolerance by regulating stomatal aperture in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central regulator of stomatal closure and osmotic stress signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by osmotic stress in microarray dataset
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The dominant negative abi1-1 mutation abolishes enhanced osmotic tolerance and enhanced stomatal closure of wrky54wrky70, showing ABA signaling is required for the phenotype
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic interaction analysis using wrky54wrky70abi1-1 triple mutant; stress phenotyping; electrolyte leakage; stomatal conductance
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: abi1-1 causes osmotic stress hypersensitivity and higher stomatal conductance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights that intact ABA signaling is necessary if manipulating WRKY genes for stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABI1-mediated ABA signaling is essential for osmotic tolerance. 2. abi1-1 suppresses the tolerant phenotype of wrky54wrky70. 3. Therefore WRKY54/WRKY70 act upstream of or through ABA-dependent stomatal control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a mechanistic common gene rather than a focal regulatory subject of the paper.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced by osmotic stress in wild type; induction reduced in wrky mutants and suppressed by SA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as a marker showing WRKY54/WRKY70 are required for full osmotic-stress gene induction, while SA suppresses this induction
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for monitoring osmotic stress and SA-ABA antagonism
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAB18 is strongly induced by PEG in wild type. 2. Its induction is reduced in wrky mutants. 3. Exogenous SA suppresses its PEG induction.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream marker gene, not a core causal determinant.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by osmotic stress in wild type; induction reduced in wrky mutants and suppressed by SA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker showing reduced osmotic stress-responsive transcription in wrky mutants and SA-mediated suppression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for stress transcriptional state
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KIN1 is PEG inducible in wild type. 2. Its induction is reduced in wrky mutants. 3. SA suppresses its osmotic induction.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream marker gene, not the main focal gene.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by osmotic stress in wild type; induction reduced in wrky mutants and suppressed by SA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker showing reduced osmotic stress-responsive transcription in wrky mutants and SA-mediated suppression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for osmotic stress transcriptional response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LTI78 is strongly induced by PEG in wild type. 2. Its induction is reduced in wrky mutants. 3. SA suppresses its PEG induction.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream marker gene, not a focal causal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Compatible osmolyte/osmoprotectant in osmotic stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the rate-limiting biosynthetic step leading from glutamate to the proline precursor delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by osmotic stress in wild type; induction reduced in wrky70 and strongly reduced in wrky54wrky70
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; explicitly described as rate-limiting for proline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced P5CS1 induction in wrky mutants, especially wrky54wrky70, is associated with abolished osmotic-stress-induced proline accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; proline measurement in mutants under PEG treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P5CS1 is an osmotic-stress-induced proline biosynthesis gene. 2. Its induction is strongly reduced in wrky70 and wrky54wrky70 mutants. 3. Reduced P5CS1 expression correlates with loss of stress-induced proline accumulation in wrky54wrky70.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance osmoprotectant accumulation, though in this paper proline accumulation did not explain the enhanced stress tolerance phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene was assayed as a downstream metabolic marker rather than the main experimental subject controlling the phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Compatible osmolyte/osmoprotectant in osmotic stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product accumulation by degrading target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the initial/rate-limiting step of proline degradation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in wrky54wrky70 even without stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; explicitly described as rate-limiting for proline catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced ProDH expression in wrky54wrky70 is associated with impaired proline accumulation under osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; proline measurement in mutants under PEG treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ProDH is a rate-limiting proline catabolic gene. 2. Its expression is elevated in wrky54wrky70 without stress. 3. Higher catabolic tendency is consistent with reduced proline accumulation in the double mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target to reduce proline loss during stress, although this study did not establish it as the causal determinant of stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assayed as a downstream metabolic marker, not a primary causal gene of the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Carotenoid cleavage step toward ABA formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates osmotic stress responses and stomatal closure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate biosynthetic control step toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced by osmotic stress in wild type; induction dramatically reduced in wrky mutants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; explicitly described as a key enzyme in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NCED3 induction is suppressed in wrky mutants, but measured ABA levels did not support a major ABA deficiency in wrky54wrky70 under stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED3 is strongly induced by osmotic stress in wild type. 2. This induction is reduced in wrky54, wrky70 and especially wrky54wrky70 mutants. 3. Despite reduced NCED3 transcript induction, ABA content was not significantly increased in the double mutant, so NCED3 suppression did not fully explain the tolerance phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important ABA-biosynthetic target for stomatal and drought engineering, but not the focal causal gene in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as a downstream ABA pathway marker rather than a core experimental driver.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and positive contribution to stomatal closure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the SA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; described as required for SA biosynthesis in sid2-1 mutant background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SID2 abolishes SA overaccumulation in wrky54wrky70 and restores osmotic-stress-inducible gene expression, but does not abolish enhanced osmotic tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis using sid2-1 crossed into wrky54wrky70; hormone measurements; stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sid2-1 prevents elevated SA and SAG accumulation in wrky54wrky70. 2. sid2-1 restores induction of osmotic-stress-responsive genes in the double-mutant background. 3. sid2-1 does not abolish the enhanced osmotic tolerance of wrky54wrky70, showing SA overaccumulation is not the main cause of tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful genetic tool to dissect SA-dependent versus SA-independent stress mechanisms; altering SA biosynthesis may affect stomatal behavior and defense tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a pathway mutant to test mechanism; not itself the main focal gene of the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates stomatal closure and osmotic stress response; SA mediates defense and contributes to stomatal closure; proline functions as osmoprotectant
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Stomatal closure and water retention during osmotic stress; indirect control of ABA/SA-related metabolic responses and proline accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; negative regulation of stomatal closure; negative feedback on SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for full osmotic induction of RAB18, LTI78, KIN1, NCED3 and P5CS1; negatively associated with stomatal closure; negatively regulates SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NCED3; P5CS1; osmotic stress-responsive genes including RAB18, LTI78, KIN1; SA biosynthesis pathway
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Osmotic stress (PEG); salicylic acid; salt; drought; cold; exogenous ABA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback on SA biosynthesis; proposed negative feedback in osmotic stress-induced stomatal control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator of osmotic stress tolerance through stomatal aperture control
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly and transiently induced by osmotic stress, peaking at 6 h; also induced by salt, drought, cold, ABA and SA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15% PEG6000 osmotic stress; drought; salt; cold; exogenous SA; exogenous ABA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WRKY70 acts as a negative regulator of stomatal closure and osmotic stress tolerance; overexpression increases wilting, electrolyte leakage and stomatal conductance, whereas loss contributes to stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; double mutant analysis; overexpression line; qRT-PCR; microarray; hormone measurements; stomatal conductance and aperture assays; water loss; electrolyte leakage; genetic interaction with sid2-1 and abi1-1
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced WRKY70 activity is associated with altered SA signaling and impaired plant defense reported by prior work; overexpression compromises osmotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing WRKY70 activity could improve drought/osmotic tolerance by enhancing stomatal closure, but potential defense and signaling tradeoffs should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY70 is rapidly induced by osmotic stress. 2. wrky70 contributes to the enhanced osmotic tolerance of the wrky54wrky70 double mutant, while WRKY70 overexpression causes hypersensitivity. 3. WRKY70 negatively regulates stomatal closure and water retention. 4. WRKY70 also contributes to induction of osmotic-stress-responsive genes and proline-related metabolism, but these are not the basis of the tolerance phenotype. 5. WRKY70 negatively regulates SA biosynthesis, yet the enhanced tolerance of wrky54wrky70 is largely SA independent.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among the two TFs, WRKY70 appears more prominent than WRKY54 because wrky70 single mutants often show intermediate phenotypes whereas wrky54 often resembles wild type.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates stomatal closure and osmotic stress response; SA mediates defense and contributes to stomatal closure; proline functions as osmoprotectant
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Stomatal closure and water retention during osmotic stress; indirect control of ABA/SA-related metabolic responses and proline accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; negative regulation of stomatal closure; negative feedback on SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Co-required with WRKY70 for full osmotic induction of RAB18, LTI78, KIN1, NCED3 and P5CS1; negatively regulates SA biosynthesis and stomatal closure
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NCED3; P5CS1; osmotic stress-responsive genes including RAB18, LTI78, KIN1; SA biosynthesis pathway
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Osmotic stress (PEG); salicylic acid; salt; drought; cold; exogenous ABA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback on SA biosynthesis; proposed negative feedback in osmotic stress-induced stomatal control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-operative negative regulator of osmotic stress tolerance through stomatal aperture control, with weaker individual effect than WRKY70
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly and transiently induced by osmotic stress, peaking at 6 h; also induced by salt, drought, cold, ABA and SA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15% PEG6000 osmotic stress; drought; salt; cold; exogenous SA; exogenous ABA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WRKY54 co-operates with WRKY70 as a negative regulator of stomatal closure and osmotic stress tolerance; its single mutant effect is weak, but the double mutant shows strong tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA single and double mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; microarray; hormone measurements; stomatal conductance and aperture assays; water loss; electrolyte leakage; genetic interaction with sid2-1 and abi1-1
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of WRKY54 together with WRKY70 alters SA signaling and may affect defense-related functions based on prior work
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be considered together with WRKY70 for engineering improved drought/osmotic tolerance through stomatal regulation, but likely requires combinatorial modification and caution about defense tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY54 is rapidly induced by osmotic stress. 2. Alone it has limited phenotypic effect, but together with WRKY70 it strongly suppresses osmotic tolerance. 3. Double loss enhances stomatal closure, water retention and stress tolerance. 4. WRKY54 also contributes to induction of osmotic-stress-responsive genes, yet those downstream responses do not explain the tolerance phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WRKY54 shows weaker individual contribution than WRKY70, but clear functional redundancy/co-operation in the double mutant.
